title: Issuing Bonds — Discount vs Premium
event: Reyes Co. issues $500,000 of 8% bonds, interest payable semiannually, maturing in 5 years. Prepare the issuance entry.

lines.label: PV of all payments at 5% semi
lines.why: Buyers pay only what the 8% coupons are worth in a 10% world.
lines.trap: Price = PV of face + PV of coupons at the MARKET rate — the stated rate only sets the coupon.

lines.label: 500,000 − 461,391
lines.why: A contra-liability: extra interest cost to be recognized over the life.
lines.trap: The discount IS deferred interest — it will flow into interest expense via amortization.

lines.label: face
lines.why: The liability is recorded at face — carrying value = face − discount.
lines.trap: Face on this line always; the discount line carries the pricing gap.

lines.label: PV at 3% semi
lines.why: Buyers pay extra for above-market coupons.
lines.trap: Direction check: generous coupon → premium; stingy coupon → discount. Flip it and everything downstream flips.

lines.label: 542,651 − 500,000
lines.why: An adjunct liability: reduces interest expense over the life.
lines.trap: Premium amortization makes expense LESS than cash paid — the mirror image of a discount.

lines.label: face
lines.trap: Carrying value = face + premium = 542,651 on day one.

memorize.body: Stated < market → discount. Stated > market → premium. The market rate prices the bond; the stated rate only writes the coupon checks.
Issue price = PV(face) + PV(coupon annuity), both at the market rate per PERIOD (semiannual: rate ÷ 2, periods × 2).

questions.prompt: $500,000 face, 8% stated, 10% market, 5 years semiannual. Issue price = ?

distractors.feedback: Face assumes the rates match. With market above stated, buyers discount the price to 461,391.
That's the PREMIUM price (6% market). When market EXCEEDS stated, the bond sells BELOW face.

build.scaffold: Cash = price. Bonds Payable = face. The gap goes to Discount (debit) or Premium (credit).
